last_completed_item: Generated and executable-validator-validated GATE-001 through GATE-010 for the Stage 1 candidate freeze, technical/developer behavioral gate, external formal round, validity audit, outcome review and explicit Stage 2 unlock; no gate sessions, human decisions or game implementation were fabricated or performed.
current_item: End-of-day human review of draft PR #13 for the validated stage1-gate batch on ai/task-split-shooter-mover-v1-continuation-8.
next_action: Review and merge the stage1-gate draft PR. In the next session, start a fresh Task Splitter context from current main, present MS-001 through MS-013 titles, owner lanes and exact dependencies for mission-state-continuity, and stop. Generate that batch only after explicit human continuation; all Stage 2 implementation remains blocked until GATE-010 is genuinely approved with stage2_unlocked=true.
resume_instruction: Read assembly/context/NEW_CHAT_RESUME.md first and follow it exactly.
blockers: Seven planned Stage 2 agent batches remain to be proposed, approved and generated one at a time.

Foundation, Contract Steward and Evidence Harness estimates total 10.9 focused lead days against the accepted S1.0 cap of 5 days; Dispatch requires human re-estimation, scope sequencing or an approved milestone-cap amendment. Evidence-critical contracts, accessibility, diagnostics, reliability and performance may not be cut to hide the overrun.
Movement-thruster estimates 6.15 focused lead days against the accepted S1.1 cap of 8 days, leaving 1.85 days for human review and bounded evidence-led iteration; Dispatch must preserve that reserve.

Combat-four-mount plus stage1-weapons consume 9.10 focused lead days of the accepted S1.2 ten-day cap, leaving only 0.90 day for review reserve and bounded approved follow-up; Dispatch may not add generalized tooling, mature balance depth or extra weapon breadth.
The three remaining Stage 2 weapon identities are deliberately deferred until Stage 1 evidence and require a later planning amendment before Stage 2 combat-content generation or dispatch.
The one remaining Stage 2 ordinary enemy role is deliberately deferred until Stage 1 evidence and requires a later planning amendment before Stage 2 combat-content generation or dispatch.
Stage1-enemies-route consumes 5.75 focused lead days of the S1.3 ten-day cap, preserving 4.25 days for human review and bounded evidence-led follow-up.
Stage1-accessibility-reliability allocates 1.70 focused lead days to S1.3 and 2.90 to S1.4, leaving 2.55 S1.3 days and 2.10 S1.4 days for review and the developer gate.
The Stage 1 gate allocates 1.90 focused lead days to S1.4 and 3.40 to S1.5, leaving 0.20 S1.4 day and 1.60 S1.5 days for review, invalid-session replacement and bounded follow-up.
GATE-010 is task decomposition only and has not been executed; no Stage 2 implementation may become ready without a genuine signed advance decision and stage2_unlocked=true.
The canonical task backlog and collaboration-state finalization remain blocked until all indexed batches validate.
Implementation and Dispatch remain blocked until the completed task-split pull request is explicitly approved and merged.
